behaviors.description: The traveling-salesman heuristic produces a route that is no more than 1.5x the length of the optimal route for inputs up to 20 stops.
behaviors.justification: This failure boundary is the heuristic algorithm’s quality bound versus an optimal solver, which can be tested in-process with deterministic coordinate fixtures. No OS/network/subprocess/LLM dependency is required.
behaviors.test_description: Run the heuristic function directly on many fixed stop sets (size <=20), compute exact optimal length for each set (or precomputed oracle sets), and assert heuristic_length/optimal_length <= 1.5.

behaviors.description: The delivery time window estimates are computed correctly from the distance between consecutive stops and the configured average speed for the road type.
behaviors.justification: Real failures are likely at module handoffs: route leg distance output + road-type speed config lookup + estimator math. A single-function unit test can miss wiring/mapping mismatches.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real local modules for leg-distance provider, road-type speed configuration, and time-window estimator; feed a multi-leg route with mixed road types and assert per-leg and total estimated times match expected calculations.

behaviors.description: The shift-hours validator rejects a route whose total estimated time exceeds the driver's remaining shift hours, returning the excess amount.
behaviors.justification: This is local validation arithmetic and decision logic on provided totals and shift hours. No external boundary is needed to expose the core failure.
behaviors.test_description: Call the validator function with estimated route durations and remaining shift hours around boundary values (equal, +1 minute, large overflow) and assert reject/accept plus exact excess-time output.

behaviors.description: When one address in the list fails geocoding (API returns no results), the service skips that stop and optimizes the remaining addresses, reporting which addresses were skipped.
behaviors.justification: The failure appears in cross-module orchestration: geocoding result handling, filtering, downstream optimization input, and skipped-address reporting. Mocking below module boundaries can hide this handoff bug.
behaviors.test_description: Use the real service pipeline with a test geocoder adapter that returns no-result for one address and valid coordinates for others; assert optimized route excludes failed address and response includes that address in skipped list.

behaviors.description: Duplicate addresses in the input list are consolidated into a single stop with combined delivery items, not visited twice.
behaviors.justification: The risk is at the boundary between dedup/consolidation logic and route construction/optimization (ensuring only one visit while preserving merged payload). Single-function tests may miss end-to-end state handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Run the local routing service modules with input containing duplicate addresses and distinct item payloads; assert one stop in optimization input/output, combined items retained, and route contains exactly one visit for that address.
